perturbation.original_text: "Annual Product Review Report" means the annual product review report as described in Title 21 of the United States Code of Federal Regulations, Section 211.180(e); "Annual Report" means the annual report as described in Title 21 of the United States Code of Federal Regulations, Section 314.81(b)(2);
perturbation.changed_text: "Annual Product Review Report" means a report summarizing product performance; "Annual Report" means a summary of yearly activities;
perturbation.explanation: By removing the specific references to the United States Code of Federal Regulations, the new definitions introduce ambiguity. While Section 6.5 requires Antares to provide information to support AMAG's regulatory filings, the altered definition makes it unclear what standards Antares must meet, creating a contradiction if compliance with specific regulations is expected elsewhere.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Antares will promptly supply on an annual basis and when reasonably requested by AMAG from time to time, at no additional charge, all available information and data in its control that AMAG reasonably requires in order to complete any filing for, or apply for, obtain or maintain, regulatory approvals under any applicable regulatory regime (including any Annual Report that AMAG is required to file with the FDA), including without limitation information relating to the Manufacturing Site, Development Report (as described in ICH guidelines), Manufacturing Services, Device, Product, sample Product, Trainers or the process, methodology, raw materials and intermediates used in the manufacture, processing, or packaging of the Device, Product, sample Product or Trainers, release test results, complaint test results, all investigations (in manufacturing, testing and storage), and all information required to be submitted in the CMC (chemistry, manufacturing and controls) section of an IND or a NDA or other regulatory filings, or required or requested to be provided to any Regulatory Authority.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes specific references to the United States Code of Federal Regulations in the definitions of 'Annual Product Review Report' and 'Annual Report'. This creates a contradiction because the 'Contradicted Text' section requires Antares to provide information and data necessary for AMAG's regulatory filings, including Annual Reports required by the FDA. The original definitions ensured clarity regarding the specific regulatory standards Antares needed to meet. By replacing these definitions with more general terms, it is now unclear what Antares must provide to satisfy AMAG's regulatory obligations. The specific regulatory standards previously defined are now ambiguous and potentially unenforceable, creating an in-text contradiction as per the 'Omissions – In Text Contradiction' definition because essential language was removed from one section and it creates a contradiction with obligations stated in another section.

perturbation.original_text: "Commercially Reasonable Efforts" means, with respect to each Party, such efforts and commitment of resources in accordance with [***] that such Party [***]. As used in this definition of "Commercially Reasonable Efforts", "reasonable" shall be measured by [***]. References in this Agreement to "commercially reasonable" and similar formulations shall be deemed to incorporate the standard set forth in this definition of "Commercially Reasonable Efforts";
perturbation.changed_text: "Commercially Reasonable Efforts" means the efforts a reasonable company would undertake under similar circumstances. References in this Agreement to "commercially reasonable" and similar formulations shall be deemed to incorporate the standard set forth in this definition of "Commercially Reasonable Efforts";
perturbation.explanation: By omitting the clause, which states "such efforts and commitment of resources in accordance with [***] that such Party [***]. As used in this definition of “Commercially Reasonable Efforts”, “reasonable” shall be measured by [***]”, it creates a vague definition of Commercial Reasonable Efforts, and creating an internal in-text contradiction within 3.6(d) where Antares is obligating itself to use Commercially Reasonable Efforts to replenish the Safety Stock.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (d) Antares or its Subcontractor shall manage the Safety Stock as part of its overall inventory and use the Safety Stock to fulfill its obligations pursuant to a Firm Orders on a first in/first out basis. As such inventory of Safety Stock is used as part of the Manufacturing Services of Product and/or sample Product, Antares shall use Commercially Reasonable Efforts to replenish the Safety Stock to the level set forth in Section 3.6(a)(i) (as adjusted pursuant to Section 3.6(a)(ii)) within [***] of receipt of such Firm Order.
perturbation.justification: The original text defined 'Commercially Reasonable Efforts' with specific metrics and requirements related to resources and measurement. The changed text removes this specific definition, replacing it with a more vague definition ('the efforts a reasonable company would undertake under similar circumstances'). This creates a contradiction with section 3.6(d), as Antares is obligated to use 'Commercially Reasonable Efforts' to replenish safety stock, and without a clear definition from the main definition clause, there's now ambiguity in the term and an in-text contradiction about what that obligation entails. This aligns with the 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction' definition because essential language was removed, creating a contradiction with obligations stated in other sections.

perturbation.original_text: "Safety Stock" has the meaning specified in Section 3.6(a);
perturbation.changed_text: "Safety Stock" means an additional quantity of components held to mitigate supply chain disruptions;
perturbation.explanation: While the original text clearly defines safety stock with respect to Section 3.6(a), the changed text makes it vague by defining safety stock as “an additional quantity of components held to mitigate supply chain disruptions”. In Section 3.6 Antares or its Subcontractor will maintain and make available to AMAG a safety inventory of the Major Device Components necessary to assemble the Devices in the quantities set forth in this Section 3.6(a) at Antares or its Subcontractor's Manufacturing Site in accordance with this Section 3.6 (“Safety Stock”).
perturbation.contradicted_text: (a) At AMAG's sole cost and expense, Antares or its Subcontractor will maintain and make available to AMAG a safety inventory of the Major Device Components necessary to assemble the Devices in the quantities set forth in this Section 3.6(a) at Antares or its Subcontractor's Manufacturing Site in accordance with this Section 3.6 ("Safety Stock").
perturbation.justification: The original text defined "Safety Stock" with reference to Section 3.6(a), which specifies the quantities and location. The changed text provides a general definition, creating ambiguity and contradicting the specificity of Section 3.6(a), where "Safety Stock" is defined with quantities at Antares or its Subcontractor's Manufacturing Site. This aligns with the 'Omissions – In Text Contradiction' definition where essential language referencing a specific section (3.6(a)) is removed, creating a contradiction with the obligations stated in Section 3.6(a).
